title: mdtoc CLI: argparse generate/check + package + integration test + README
description: Owns projects/mdtoc/mdtoc/cli.py, mdtoc/__main__.py, mdtoc/__init__.py, tests/test_cli.py, tests/test_integration.py, tests/fixtures/sample.md, projects/mdtoc/README.md. Wire parser -> promoted slugger -> inserter behind: python3 -m mdtoc generate FILE [--max-depth N] [--in-place] and python3 -m mdtoc check FILE (exit 0 fresh, 1 stale). Author integration test on a fixture exercising code fences, duplicate + unicode headings, and idempotency; write README with usage examples. Stdlib only.
acceptance_criteria: Owns EXACTLY: projects/mdtoc/mdtoc/cli.py, projects/mdtoc/mdtoc/__main__.py, projects/mdtoc/mdtoc/__init__.py, projects/mdtoc/tests/test_cli.py, projects/mdtoc/tests/test_integration.py, projects/mdtoc/tests/fixtures/sample.md, projects/mdtoc/README.md. Depends on T-021 (parser), T-022 (inserter), T-027 (promoted slugger.py).

cli.py wires the pipeline: parser.parse_headings -> render TOC via inserter.render_toc(headings, slugger.slugify, max_depth) -> inserter.insert_toc. __init__.py makes mdtoc a regular package; __main__.py calls cli.main so `python3 -m mdtoc ...` works.

Command `python3 -m mdtoc generate FILE [--max-depth N] [--in-place]`: default --max-depth 3. If the toc markers are present: insert/replace the TOC (idempotently); with --in-place write the file back, otherwise print the updated file to stdout. If markers are ABSENT: print just the rendered TOC to stdout.

Command `python3 -m mdtoc check FILE`: exit 0 if the TOC between the markers is already fresh (regenerating yields identical content), exit 1 if stale. Clean argparse --help for the program and both subcommands.

tests/fixtures/sample.md: a markdown fixture exercising a fenced code block containing '#' lines, an HTML comment containing a '#' line, duplicate headings (dedupe), unicode headings (accented + CJK), and the toc markers.
test_cli.py: unit tests that invoke cli.main on temp copies of the fixture - generate --in-place, check returns 0 when fresh and 1 after tampering, and --max-depth truncation.

test_integration.py: end-to-end on the fixture - running generate --in-place TWICE yields a byte-identical file (idempotency), the fenced/comment '#' lines never appear in the TOC, duplicate headings get -1/-2 anchors, and check returns 0 afterward.
README.md: usage + copy-pasteable examples for generate (both modes) and check.
All suites pass via `python3 -m unittest discover -s projects/mdtoc/tests -t projects/mdtoc`; stdlib only.

SHARED CONVENTIONS (all mdtoc tasks): Python 3.9+ stdlib ONLY, no pip deps. Package dir projects/mdtoc/mdtoc/ (namespace package; __init__.py is created only by T-028, so test files must add projects/mdtoc/ to sys.path and import as `from mdtoc.parser import ...`). Tests use unittest and must pass via `python3 -m unittest discover -s projects/mdtoc/tests -t projects/mdtoc`. No file listed here may be created/edited by any other task (disjoint ownership).

notes.note: plan: build cli.py wiring parser->slugger->inserter with generate/check subcommands + argparse; add __init__.py + __main__.py; write fixture sample.md exercising fences/comments/dup/unicode headings + markers; write test_cli.py + test_integration.py (idempotency, tamper-detect, max-depth truncation); write README.md; run goal_mode bounded loop + manual e2e

EXPECTED: wire parser->slugger->inserter behind argparse generate/check per acceptance criteria, all criteria bullets satisfied. ACTUAL: goal_mode.py run --task T-028 --cmd 'python3 -m unittest discover -s projects/mdtoc/tests -t projects/mdtoc -v' -> GOAL REACHED iteration 1/3 (0.28s), 62/62 tests pass (18 new in test_cli.py+test_integration.py, 44 pre-existing parser/inserter/slugger untouched). Manual e2e from repo root confirmed: (1) PYTHONPATH=projects/mdtoc python3 -m mdtoc generate SCRATCH/e2e_sample.md -> stdout mode prints full doc with TOC spliced, exit 0, file untouched; (2) check on fresh copy (markers present but empty) -> 'TOC is stale', exit 1 (expected: empty != regenerated); (3) generate --in-place -> exit 0; (4) check afterward -> 'TOC is up to date', exit 0; (5) generate --in-place AGAIN -> diff against step-3 output showed zero bytes changed (byte-identical/idempotent); (6) tampered an anchor via python string replace, check -> 'TOC is stale', exit 1. --help clean for mdtoc/generate/check (argparse, exit 0, mentions generate/check/--max-depth/--in-place/FILE). No deviations from acceptance criteria; only design choices made where criteria were silent: markers-absent+--in-place prints a stderr warning then falls back to TOC-only stdout (exit 0); check has no --max-depth flag (uses default 3, matching literal criteria wording); check with absent markers exits 1 with stderr message. Files created match 'Owns EXACTLY' list verbatim (git status --short confirms exactly cli.py, __main__.py, __init__.py, test_cli.py, test_integration.py, fixtures/sample.md, README.md as untracked, no other project files touched).

HANDOFF -> verifier: REPLAY: (1) python3 -m unittest discover -s projects/mdtoc/tests -t projects/mdtoc -v  -> expect 'Ran 62 tests ... OK' (18 new: test_cli.py TestGenerateInPlace/TestGenerateStdoutMode/TestMaxDepthTruncation/TestCheck/TestArgparseHelp + test_integration.py TestEndToEndIdempotencyAndFidelity, plus 44 pre-existing parser/inserter/slugger tests untouched). (2) cd projects/mdtoc && python3 -m mdtoc --help / generate --help / check --help -> exit 0, clean argparse usage mentioning generate/check/--max-depth/--in-place/FILE. (3) cp projects/mdtoc/tests/fixtures/sample.md /tmp/sample.md && cd projects/mdtoc && python3 -m mdtoc check /tmp/sample.md -> exit 1 'TOC is stale' (markers present but empty). (4) python3 -m mdtoc generate /tmp/sample.md --in-place -> exit 0; python3 -m mdtoc check /tmp/sample.md -> exit 0 'TOC is up to date'. (5) cp /tmp/sample.md /tmp/sample_after1.md && python3 -m mdtoc generate /tmp/sample.md --in-place && diff /tmp/sample_after1.md /tmp/sample.md -> no diff (byte-identical / idempotent, confirmed independently by test_integration.test_generate_in_place_twice_is_byte_identical via subprocess). (6) sed -i '' 's/#introduction)/#introduction-tampered)/' /tmp/sample.md && python3 -m mdtoc check /tmp/sample.md -> exit 1 'TOC is stale'. (7) python3 -m mdtoc generate /tmp/sample.md (no --in-place) -> prints full doc with TOC to stdout, file on disk unchanged. Checklist vs T-028.json acceptance_criteria: [1] owns exactly cli.py/__main__.py/__init__.py/test_cli.py/test_integration.py/fixtures/sample.md/README.md (verified via git status --short projects/mdtoc, no other files touched, parser.py/inserter.py/slugger.py from T-021/T-022/T-027 read-only/unmodified) - MET. [2] cli.py wires parser.parse_headings -> inserter.render_toc(headings, slugger.slugify, max_depth) -> inserter.insert_toc; __init__.py present; __main__.py calls cli.main - MET (see mdtoc/cli.py _render()/_cmd_generate()/_cmd_check()). [3] generate command: default --max-depth 3, markers present -> idempotent insert (in-place writes file / else prints full doc to stdout), markers absent -> prints only rendered TOC to stdout - MET, verified by test_cli.TestGenerateStdoutMode.test_without_markers_prints_only_the_rendered_toc and manual e2e step 7. [4] check command: exit 0 fresh / 1 stale, clean --help for program+both subcommands - MET. [5] fixture exercises fenced code block '#' line, HTML comment '#' line, duplicate headings (## Cafe x2), unicode headings (accented Cafe + CJG 你好世界), toc markers - MET (projects/mdtoc/tests/fixtures/sample.md). [6] test_cli.py unit tests: generate --in-place, check 0-then-1-after-tamper, --max-depth truncation - MET (TestGenerateInPlace, TestCheck, TestMaxDepthTruncation). [7] test_integration.py e2e: generate --in-place TWICE byte-identical, fenced/comment lines never in TOC, duplicate headings get -1/-2 anchors, check 0 afterward - MET (subprocess-based, see test_generate_in_place_twice_is_byte_identical, test_duplicate_headings_get_dash1_dash2_anchors, test_check_returns_zero_after_generate). [8] README.md usage + copy-pasteable examples for generate (both modes) and check - MET (projects/mdtoc/README.md, includes both PYTHONPATH and cwd invocation styles as required by task step 5). [9] all suites pass via python3 -m unittest discover -s projects/mdtoc/tests -t projects/mdtoc, stdlib only (argparse/subprocess/unicodedata/re/os/sys/shutil/tempfile/contextlib/io - all stdlib) - MET. [10] shared conventions (3.9+ stdlib only, disjoint ownership, tests add projects/mdtoc to sys.path) - MET. DEVIATIONS/judgment calls where criteria were silent: markers-absent+--in-place combo prints stderr warning then falls back to TOC-only stdout with exit 0 (not tested by acceptance criteria explicitly, documented in cli.py docstring); check has no --max-depth flag, always uses default 3 (matches literal criteria text 'python3 -m mdtoc check FILE'); check with markers fully absent exits 1 with stderr diagnostic (undefined by criteria, chosen as safe default). goal_mode.py run --task T-028 --cmd '...' -> GOAL REACHED iteration 1/3, no failures encountered, no retries needed.
VERIFYING: replay full suite + README-exact e2e on scratchpad copies + cognitive-diversity probes + max-depth sharp-edge repro + discipline audit via events.jsonl/git diff

VERIFIED (replayed independently, not trusting logs): (1) full suite my own run 'Ran 62 tests in 0.239s OK' = 62/62. (2) README-exact e2e on scratchpad copy of committed fixture (committed fixture untouched, sha 5745f5b...): stdout mode prints full doc+TOC exit0 file-untouched; check-on-empty-markers exit1; generate --in-place exit0; check exit0; second --in-place byte-identical (sha 9bc87b78... both runs); tamper #introduction -> check exit1. Emitted TOC byte-matches README worked-example block. (3) Cognitive-diversity probes (my own crafted inputs, static read of cli.py): unicode/dedup cafe->#cafe/#cafe-1/#cafe-2 + CJK 你好世界/日本語 preserved; backtick+tilde fence '#' lines and HTML-comment '#' line all excluded; --max-depth 2 vs default 3 changes output (drops H3/H4); empty file gen exit0/check exit1 clean; markers+zero-headings gen&check exit0 'up to date'; nonexistent path -> 'mdtoc: error: [Errno 2]...' exit2 no traceback. (4) --help clean exit0 for mdtoc/generate/check (mentions generate/check/--max-depth/--in-place/FILE). Deviations judged: (a) --in-place markers-absent -> stderr warning + stdout TOC-only exit0, file untouched, matches README example verbatim - ACCEPT. (b) check has no --max-depth, hard-coded depth 3. SHARP EDGE REPRODUCED: generate --in-place --max-depth 2 on doc WITH level-3 heading yields valid depth-2 TOC, then check reports STALE exit1 (control w/o depth-3 heading -> exit0). RULING: DOCUMENTED LIMITATION, not a trap - README line103 explicitly states check 'Recomputes the TOC (at the default --max-depth 3)', and literal acceptance criteria specify 'check FILE' with no depth flag, so check@3 is contract-consistent; no crash/corruption. Non-blocking recommendation: add explicit callout in check --help. ACCEPT. (c) check markers-absent -> exit1 clean diagnostic - ACCEPT. (5) README both invocation styles work from fresh shells: Option A cwd=projects/mdtoc (no PYTHONPATH) and Option B PYTHONPATH=projects/mdtoc both exit0; examples match real output. (6) DISCIPLINE via events.jsonl: exactly 7 locks acquired 07:42:23 (before writes 07:46 / handoff 07:47:51), all 7 released 07:47:57 (after); handoff via task_handoff NOT self-done (producer=substrate-worker-1/main, approver=harness-verifier); upstream parser.py(T-021)/inserter.py(T-022)/slugger.py(T-027) UNMODIFIED (git diff HEAD empty). Owns EXACTLY the 7 artifacts (git status; fixtures/ holds only sample.md). Stdlib-only imports confirmed. All 10 acceptance criteria MET.
